meta.title: Ranuk Orbit — Earth, from another angle
meta.description: Cinematic aerial photography & travel storytelling by Emilio Ranucoli. Drone, Ray-Ban Meta POV, editorial work from the Mediterranean, the Andes, the medina and the snow line.

hero.sub: A film and stills practice rooted in real places — the Mediterranean, the Andes, the medina, the snow line.

story.title: Showing what I'm lucky enough to see.
story.sub: Why I started capturing images, and what I look for in every one.
story.p1: I'm a lucky one. I've had the chance to travel, and from the first trip I felt something close to a debt — that what I was seeing didn't belong to me alone. I capture images so the person who can't be there gets to live it a little too.
story.p2: Before the camera, there's the eye. No gear replaces that. Before each photo or video I stop: I read the context, what the landscape is offering, what the moment has that's unrepeatable. If that reading doesn't come, better not to shoot — the image won't transmit anything.
story.p3: The drone was a shift in perspective. As a kid I dreamt of flying, and somehow that dream came true through the controller. But more than altitude, what it gave me was another way to read the same place — the line of the coast, the pattern of the forest, what the land says when you pull away from it.
story.p4: Today I combine air and ground: the drone for the composition only seen from above, the Ray-Ban Meta for the presence only felt walking. Every project starts with the same question — what does this place want to say, and how do I tell it without a postcard?
story.pull: A good image starts in the eyes, not in the camera.

services.title: Three packages, one handshake.
services.sub: Pricing in EUR. Travel quoted separately.
services.processSub: Same four steps, every project. No surprises in the middle.

pov.title: At eye level.
pov.sub: No composition, no framing. Only presence.
